facts: Barbados is a vibrant Caribbean island nation located in the Atlantic Ocean, known as the birthplace of rum, its British-influenced culture, and stunning white-sand beaches. As a sovereign republic (since 2021) with a population of approximately 290,000, it is a densely populated hub of tourism, cricket, and calypso music, with Bridgetown serving as its capital.
name: BA
history: Barbados History - A History of The Island of BarbadosBarbados has a rich history spanning from early Amerindian habitation to becoming a modern republic in 2021. Settled by the British in 1627, it became a wealthy sugar plantation economy reliant on enslaved African labor until 1834. It achieved independence in 1966 and transitioned from a constitutional monarchy to a republic on November 30, 2021.
